citations.description: Epic ticket status is 'in_progress'; child stories 6fe2, e622, 568c show status='closed' per show_ticket, with completion-verifier SHAs recorded in their attestations, but git log output is not accessible via file tools.

findings.criterion: Each child ticket trailer is present on origin/main after the gated feature-branch merge. Prove with git log origin/main --format=%B | grep -q "rebar-ticket: 6fe2-ab22-c231-4a41" && git log origin/main --format=%B | grep -q "rebar-ticket: e622-a596-3ceb-4f74" && git log origin/main --format=%B | grep -q "rebar-ticket: 568c-b44b-7e2b-4d87".
findings.detail: This criterion requires inspecting git log on origin/main for the presence of specific rebar-ticket trailers for the three child stories (6fe2, e622, 568c). The codebase file tools cannot execute git commands, so the presence of these trailers on origin/main cannot be verified. The epic status is still in_progress, which is consistent with the feature-branch merge not yet being reflected in the store's closure state. The child stories are individually closed and have completion-verifier signatures at specific commit SHAs (03fda50fb for 6fe2, 55606b8e5 for e622, 4dd9172b9 for 568c), but whether those commits or a subsequent no-ff merge change carries the required trailers on origin/main cannot be confirmed through file reads alone.
findings.remediation: Run: git log origin/main --format=%B | grep -q 'rebar-ticket: 6fe2-ab22-c231-4a41' && git log origin/main --format=%B | grep -q 'rebar-ticket: e622-a596-3ceb-4f74' && git log origin/main --format=%B | grep -q 'rebar-ticket: 568c-b44b-7e2b-4d87'. If any grep fails, the feature-branch no-ff merge change has not yet been submitted to main. Complete the gated merge per the epic's Gerrit workflow (feature/creation-channel-provenance → main) and confirm each child trailer is present.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
